- Insertion at the perineal body supports pelvic floor structures.

Key evidence:
- Levator ani involves multiple muscle segments beyond just iliococcygeus and pubococcygeus.
- Complete description must include puborectalis for accuracy.

- Levator ani originates broadly across different bone surfaces rather than focusing narrowly on certain regions.
- Its function requires inclusion of the puborectalis muscle along with iliococcygeus and pubococcygeus.
